query: Blue Origin New Glenn NG-2 ESCAPADE first stage landing Jacklyn loiter orbit mission date 2026

{"query":"Blue Origin New Glenn NG-2 ESCAPADE first stage landing Jacklyn loiter orbit mission date 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1609,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.blueorigin.com/missions/ng-2","title":"NG-2 | Missions | Blue Origin","content":"The New Glenn orbital launch vehicle successfully completed its second mission on November 13, 2025, deploying NASA’s Escape and Plasma Acceleration and Dynamics Explorers (ESCAPADE) twin-spacecraft into the designated loiter orbit, and landing the fully reusable first stage on Jacklyn in the Atlantic Ocean."},{"url":"https://spacenews.com/new-glenn-launches-nasas-escapade-mars-mission-lands-booster/","title":"New Glenn launches NASA’s ESCAPADE Mars mission, lands booster - SpaceNews","content":"WASHINGTON — Blue Origin successfully launched a NASA Mars mission on the second flight of its New Glenn booster Nov.
The booster’s first stage, called “Never Tell Me The Odds” by Blue Origin, shut down its seven BE-4 engines and separated from the upper stage a little more than three minutes after liftoff, then attempted a landing on Blue Origin’s landing ship, Jacklyn, in the Atlantic Ocean.
The twin spacecraft separated about 30 seconds apart a little more than 33 minutes after liftoff, after a second burn of the New Glenn upper stage.
Dave Curtis, ESCAPADE project manager, said at a Nov.
NG-1 launched in January 2025 with a Blue Origin demonstration payload that remained attached to the upper stage.
2 more free articles this month."},{"url":"https://www.blueorigin.com/news/new-glenn-launches-nasa-escapade-lands-fully-reusable-booster","title":"New Glenn Launches NASA’s ESCAPADE, Lands Fully Reusable Booster | Blue Origin","content":"Nov 13, 2025 — landing the fully reusable first stage on Jacklyn … on Thursday, November 13, 2025, on May 28, 2026. launch no earlier than Sunday, April 19, ...
New Glenn’s seven BE-4 engines ignited on Thursday, November 13, 2025, at 3:55:01 PM EST / 20:55:01 UTC from Launch Complex 36 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station.
“It turns out Never Tell Me The Odds had perfect odds—never before in history has a booster this large nailed the landing on the second try.
"Today was a tremendous achievement for the New Glenn team, opening a new era for Blue Origin and the industry as we look to launch, land, repeat, again and again," said Jordan Charles, Vice President, New Glenn.
A note from CEO Dave Limp: On May 28, 2026, we experienced a significant anomaly during a New Glenn Integrated Launch Vehicle hotfire test."},{"url":"https://spaceflightnow.com/2025/11/13/blue-origin-launches-twin-mars-probes-for-nasa-as-new-glenn-makes-first-landing/","title":"Blue Origin launches twin Mars probes for NASA as New Glenn makes first landing – Spaceflight Now","content":"The centerpiece of Amazon- and Blue Origin-founder Jeff Bezos’ space ambitious, the towering 321-foot tall New Glenn rocket’s seven methane-burning main engines ignited at 3:55 p.m.
The New Glenn’s maiden flight last January successfully boosted a Blue Origin payload into orbit, but the reusable first stage failed in its attempt to reach an off-shore landing ship, named after Bezos’ mother Jacklyn.
The 188-foot-tall first stage launched Thursday, nicknamed “Never Tell Me The Odds,” featured a variety of upgrades to improve performance.
The next such window opens in 2026.
The probes were deployed on a trajectory that will carry them a million miles out, well past the moon’s orbit, where they will loiter for the next 11 months before heading back toward Earth."}]}

Glenn Launches NASA’s ESCAPADE, Lands Fully Reusable Booster | Blue Origin","content":"Blue Origin: For The Benefit of Earth. **Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, Fla., (November 13, 2025)**—The New Glenn orbital launch vehicle successfully completed its second mission, deploying NASA’s Escape and Plasma Acceleration and Dynamics Explorers (ESCAPADE) twin-spacecraft into the designated loiter orbit, and landing the fully reusable first stage on Jacklyn in the Atlantic Ocean. In addition to deploying the NASA spacecraft, the Viasat HaloNet demonstration onboard New Glenn’s second stage successfully executed the first flight test of Viasat’s telemetry data relay service for NASA’s Communications Services Project. “Congratulations to Blue Origin, Rocket Lab, UC Berkeley, and all of our partners on the successful launch of ESCAPADE,\" said the acting NASA Administrator, Secretary Sean Duffy. All of this information will be critical to protect future NASA explorers and invaluable as we evaluate how to deliver on President Trump’s vision of planting the Stars and Stripes on Mars.”. Blue Origin is certifying New Glenn with the U.S. Space Force for the NSSL program to meet emerging national security objectives.","score":0.63234437,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://spaceflightnow.com/2025/11/13/blue-origin-launches-twin-mars-probes-for-nasa-as-new-glenn-makes-first-landing","title":"Blue Origin launches twin Mars probes for NASA as New Glenn makes first landing – Spaceflight Now","content":"* [[ July 1, 2026 ]   SpaceX launches 24 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg SFB   Falcon 9](https://spaceflightnow.com/2026/07/01/live-coverage-spacex-to-launch-24-starlink-satellites-on-falcon-9-rocket-from-vandenberg-sfb-9/ \"SpaceX launches 24 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg SFB\"). * [[ June 30, 2026 ]   Blue Origin outlines return to flight logistics for its New Glenn rockets   Moon Base](https://spaceflightnow.com/2026/06/30/blue-origin-outlines-return-to-flight-logistics-for-its-new-glenn-rockets/ \"Blue Origin outlines return to flight logistics for its New Glenn rockets\"). # Blue Origin launches twin Mars probes for NASA as New Glenn makes first landing. Image: Michael Cain/Spaceflight Now. Blue Origin launched its second heavy-lift New Glenn rocket Thursday, putting two small NASA satellites onto a long, looping course to Mars to learn more about how the sun has slowly blown away the red planet’s once thick atmosphere. Much like returning SpaceX Falcon 9 rockets, the larger New Glenn booster will be hauled back to Port Canaveral and, depending on its condition, be refurbished and readied for use on an upcoming New Glenn flight.","score":0.61834323,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.blueorigin.com/missions/ng-2","title":"NG-2 | Missions | Blue Origin","content":"Blue Origin: For The Benefit of Earth. # New Glenn Mission NG-2. The New Glenn orbital launch vehicle successfully completed its second mission on November 13, 2025, deploying NASA’s Escape and Plasma Acceleration and Dynamics Explorers (ESCAPADE) twin-spacecraft into the designated loiter orbit, and landing the fully reusable first stage on Jacklyn in the Atlantic Ocean. ## NG-2 Flight Profile. Meanwhile, the two BE-3U engines ignite, propelling New Glenn’s second stage into space. After completion of the mission profile, the second stage will be safed and inerted, which is compliant with NASA’s Orbital Debris Mitigation Standard Practices. A diagram of New Glenn 2’s flight profile from liftoff at LC-36, through ascent, stage separation, fairing jettison, and payload separation. ### Blue Origin Images. Images in Blue Origin's gallery are available for download. ### Shop New Glenn. Share your passion for space with new gear from the Blue Origin Shop. Sign up to receive updates on Blue Origin's announcements, launches, and opportunities.","score":0.54724693,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.96,"request_id":"eda8e621-6899-4ddb-9d0b-952a9ff80af8"}
